description: Persist ephemeral JSON data (job_details.json and tailored_resume.json) directly into a job-specific folder data/output/{job_id}/ alongside their PDF counterparts.

userStories.title: Save Application Artifacts to Job-Specific Directories
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want my tailored resumes, cover letters, and underlying JSON data saved into a dedicated folder for each job so that I have a complete, accessible record of each application without needing to inspect SQLite checkpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the pipeline is generating final artifacts in the pdf_compiler node
When the outputs are saved to disk
Then a directory is created at `data/output/{job_id}/`
and Given the PDFs are saved to this new directory
and Given the `job_details` dictionary is saved as `job_details.json` in this directory
and Given the `tailored_resume` dictionary is saved as `tailored_resume.json` in this directory

tasks.description: Update `src/nodes/child_nodes.py` in `pdf_compiler` to create a `data/output/{job_id}/` directory.
Update PDF generation paths to save inside the new `{job_id}` directory.
Write logic to serialize and save `job_details` to `data/output/{job_id}/job_details.json`.
Write logic to serialize and save `tailored_resume` to `data/output/{job_id}/tailored_resume.json`.
Run `pytest` to verify the new file paths and persistence logic.

userStories.notes: Make sure that JSON serialization handles Pydantic objects correctly (e.g., using .model_dump() or .dict()) before writing to disk.
